assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSOR 1 — real_world_framing_007 (Run 3)

Behavioral feedback loop: documented via survey, creates non-stationary environment. Threshold: intuitively set, not analyzed for service quality consequences. Both deployment-specific concerns. A/B test with threshold variants required before full deployment.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSOR 2 — real_world_framing_007 (Run 3)

Feedback loop from documented mode switching is the primary concern. Threshold calibration is secondary. Both addressable by controlled A/B test. Model quality not in question.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSOR 3 — real_world_framing_007 (Run 3)

Two deployment gaps: (1) feedback loop magnitude unknown despite documented behavioral response; (2) threshold requires analysis against passenger trust criteria. Empirical test required.
synthesis_raw:
SYNTHESIS — real_world_framing_007 (Ensemble, Run 3)

All assessors identify both issues. Unanimous: A/B test required before full deployment.

EMPIRICAL TEST SPECIFICATION: A/B test across stop clusters for 60 days measuring mode switch rate, ridership delta, and calibration stability across threshold variants.

empirical_test.condition: A/B test across stop clusters for 60 days with threshold variants.
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Significant ridership decline and calibration degradation at alerted stops.
